rationales.hook_strength: 'Plain water isn't hydrating you' is a specific, counterintuitive claim that earns the stop — loses a point because the sub-headline over-explains before the reader swipes.
rationales.clarity: Each slide is a single, actionable idea with a clear label and one concrete takeaway — no bloat.
rationales.audience_fit: Afternoon brain fog, chugging water with no result, a dead-simple fix — this is written directly at the tired 20–35 who already thinks they're doing the right thing.
rationales.novelty: Electrolytes-in-water is a real insight but has become near-ubiquitous on health TikTok in the last two years — the pinch-of-salt angle is familiar.
rationales.brand_fit: Voice is plain and direct, claims are appropriately hedged ('can all point to'), and the CTA ties naturally to Vibe's score mechanic — loses a point because 'hydrate so it actually counts' edges toward the kind of polished tagline that feels slightly off-brand.
rationales.predicted_performance: High save potential because it's practical and specific, but the saturated electrolyte topic will cap organic reach unless the hook visual is strong enough to distinguish it in feed.
